name: S-46 (SS-157)
events: 5 Mar 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) departed from Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone for Brisbane, Australia.
15 Apr 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) arrived at Brisbane.
13 May 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) departed from Brisbane for her 1st war patrol. She was ordered to patrol off New Britain.
21 Jun 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) ended her 1st war patrol at Brisbane.
20 Jul 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) departed from Brisbane for her 2nd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol in the Solomons.
15 Aug 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. R.C. Lynch, Jr.) ended her 2nd war patrol at Brisbane.
11 Sep 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. E.R. Crawford) departed from Brisbane for her 3rd war patrol. She was ordered to patrol east of New Guinea.
11 Oct 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. E.R. Crawford) ended her 3rd war patrol at Brisbane.
4 Nov 1942 USS S-46 (Lt. E.R. Crawford) departed from Brisbane to return to the Panama Canal Zone.

9 Jan 1943 USS S-46 (Lt. E.R. Crawford) arrived at Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone. She is now sent to Philadelphia on the US east coast for an overhaul. After overhaul S-46 was sent to Alaska where she made two war patrols in late 1943 - early 1944.
8 Feb 1943 USS S-46 departed Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone for St. Thomas, Virgin Islands.
14 Feb 1943 USS S-46 arrived at St. Thomas, Virgin Islands from Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone.
25 Mar 1943 USS S-46 departed Trinidad for the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
3 Apr 1943 USS S-46 arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard from Trinidad for a refit.
14 Jun 1943 With her refit completed, USS S-46, departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard for Hampton Roads, Viginia.
21 Jun 1943 USS S-46 departed Hampton Roads, Viginia for Coco Solo, Panama Canal Zone.
